mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which directly translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: If RSAFS shows consistent growth but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of such instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals a severe deterioration in the labor market, leading to reduced consumer spending, lower corporate profits, and a subsequent decline in S&P 500 valuations over the medium term.
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months, but S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of such occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of persistently high inflation (CPIAUCSL annual change > 3%) and an aggressive increase in the federal funds rate signifies a strong monetary tightening cycle, which typically leads to higher discount rates, reduced corporate profitability, and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL annual change > 3% AND FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25pp in 3 months
regime_hint: inflationary environment with tightening monetary policy
failure_condition: If both CPIAUCSL annual change exceeds 3% and FEDFUNDS rises by more than 0.25 percentage points over 3 months, yet S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-recession recovery, robust annualized GDP growth exceeding 2% indicates a strong rebound in overall economic activity, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives S&P 500 appreciation over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 growth consistently exceeds 2% QoQ annualized during recovery periods, but S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 45% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production greater than 0.5% signals an acceleration in manufacturing and mining output, indicating a strengthening supply side of the economy that typically correlates with positive short-term equity market performance.
failure_condition: If INDPRO rises by more than 0.5% MoM, but S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are negative in more than 55% of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent positive growth in nonfarm payrolls signifies a healthy and expanding labor market, which underpins consumer confidence and spending, thereby supporting corporate revenues and leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS shows consistent positive growth but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of such instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts combined with an increase in the federal funds rate indicates a significant slowdown in the housing sector and broader economic tightening, which typically precedes or accompanies economic contractions and leads to negative S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls > 10% MoM AND FEDFUNDS rises > 0.1pp in 1 month
failure_condition: If HOUST falls by more than 10% MoM while FEDFUNDS rises by more than 0.1 percentage points in the same month, yet S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is maintained at extremely low levels (below 0.25%), it signals an accommodative monetary policy stance, encouraging risk-taking and providing liquidity, which tends to support short-term positive S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Federal Reserve's near-zero interest rate policy post-2008 crisis
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25% but S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are negative in more than 55% of such periods.
